title: Aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut
abstract: An aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut (50) comprising: an outer cylinder (26) having an inner surface defining a cylinder bore extending into the outer cylinder from a first axial face of the outer cylinder, the cylinder bore defining an oleo-pneumatic chamber containing a damping liquid and a gas which can mix within the cylinder bore; a sliding tube (28) including a first end portion movably mounted within the cylinder bore so as to be movable along the cylinder bore between a compressed position, in which a second end portion of the sliding tube disposed outside of the bore is relatively close to the first axial face of the outer cylinder, and an extended position, in which the second end portion of the sliding tube is relatively far from the first axial face of the outer cylinder, the sliding tube including a tube bore extending into the sliding tube from the first end portion, the tube bore being arranged in fluid communication with the cylinder bore to exchange the damping liquid with the cylinder bore; an elongate metering pin (34) having a longitudinal axis and including an axial channel for passage of damping liquid as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position; and a flow restricting plate (52) including: a first portion (52a) provided with a first damping orifice (DO) in which the metering pin is located and through which the metering pin axially moves as the sliding tube moves between the extended and compressed positions to vary the free space cross sectional area of the first damping orifice to control an axial flow of the damping liquid (J) moving from the tube bore to the cylinder bore as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position; and a second portion (52b) provided with a second damping orifice (DO2) for passage of damping liquid as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position to define a second flow of the damping liquid (J2), wherein the second damping orifice is orientated to direct the second flow of damping liquid towards the axial channel of the metering pin to disrupt the axial flow of damping liquid.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut comprising: an outer cylinder having an inner surface defining a cylinder bore extending into the outer cylinder from a first axial face of the outer cylinder, the cylinder bore defining an oleo-pneumatic chamber containing a damping liquid and a gas which can mix within the cylinder bore; a sliding tube including a first end portion movably mounted within the cylinder bore so as to be movable along the cylinder bore between a compressed position, in which a second end portion of the sliding tube disposed outside of the bore is relatively close to the first axial face of the outer cylinder, and an extended position, in which the second end portion of the sliding tube is relatively far from the first axial face of the outer cylinder, the sliding tube including a tube bore extending into the sliding tube from the first end portion, the tube bore being arranged in fluid communication with the cylinder bore to exchange the damping liquid with the cylinder bore; an elongate metering pin having a longitudinal axis and including an axial channel for passage of damping liquid as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position; and a flow restricting plate including: a first portion provided with a first damping orifice in which the metering pin is located and through which the metering pin axially moves as the sliding tube moves between the extended and compressed positions to vary the free space cross sectional area of the first damping orifice to control an axial flow of the damping liquid moving from the tube bore to the cylinder bore as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position; and a second portion provided with a second damping orifice for passage of damping liquid as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position to define a second flow of the damping liquid, wherein the second damping orifice is orientated to direct the second flow of damping liquid towards the axial channel of the metering pin to disrupt the axial flow of damping liquid. 2. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to claim 1, wherein the axis of the second damping orifice is at an angle of at least 15 degrees with respect to the axis of the first damping orifice. 3. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to claim 2, wherein the axis of the second damping orifice is at an angle of 15 degrees to 90 degrees with respect to the axis of the first damping orifice. 4. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim, wherein the flow restricting plate is defined by an end portion of an orifice support tube, the office support tube being coupled to the outer cylinder to maintain the flow restricting plate at a fixed distance from a second axial face of the outer cylinder, the orifice support tube having a sidewall provided with a plurality of fluid passage holes. 5. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim, wherein the metering pin has a circular cross section and the axial channel has a base defined by a chord of the circular cross section, such that the axial channel has open sides to enable the second flow of damping liquid to disrupt the axial flow of damping liquid moving along the channel. 6. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim, wherein the first portion and the second portion of the flow restricting plate are parallel and the second orifice has an inlet and outlet of a first diameter and includes a central portion of reduced diameter with respect to the inlet and outlet. 7. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the second portion of the flow restricting plate is non-parallel with respect to the first portion and the axis of the second orifice is normal with respect to the second portion. 8. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim, further comprising a baffle plate including a plurality of axial fluid passages and positioned in a fixed relationship with respect to the flow restricting plate such that the axial flow of damping liquid strikes the baffle plate once disrupted by the second flow of damping liquid. 9. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim when dependent on claim 4, wherein the orifice support tube comprises a curved surface positioned in a fixed relationship with respect to the flow restricting plate such that the axial flow of damping liquid strikes the curved surface once disrupted by the 

second flow of damping liquid, the curved surface being curved away from the axis of the metering pin to diffuse the disrupted axial flow of damping liquid. 10. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to claim 9, wherein the curved surface directs the disrupted axial flow of damping liquid to a fluid passage hole through the orifice support tube. 11. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the curved surface is defined as a vane and optionally the vane is defined by a tube. 12. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to claim 11, wherein the tube widens away from the metering pin to reduce the velocity of the fluid flow and/or includes projections extending into the tube in order to dissipate the fluid jet energy in turbulence. 13. The aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim, wherein the flow restricting plate includes one or more further portions each provided with a further damping orifice for passage of damping liquid as the sliding tube moves from the extended position towards the compressed position to define a further flow of the damping liquid, each further damping orifice being orientated to direct the further flow of damping liquid towards the or a further axial channel of the metering pin to disrupt the or a further axial flow of damping liquid. 14. An aircraft landing gear assembly comprising: the aircraft landing gear shock absorber strut according to any preceding claim; a wheel or other ground contacting assembly coupled to the shock absorber strut; and a mounting bearing via which the aircraft landing gear assembly is arranged to be movably coupled to an aircraft for movement between a stowed condition for flight and a deployed condition for take-off and landing. 15. The aircraft landing gear assembly according to claim 14, further comprising a side stay, drag stay or plunger lock arrangement coupled to the shock absorber strut and arranged to enable the shock absorber strut to be maintained in the deployed condition.
